message:
perf(columnar): eliminate redundant identity casts in scalar coercion hot path

Two optimizations to the scalar casting/coercion pipeline:

fp-columnar: when values already match the target dtype and no coercion
is needed, skip calling cast_scalar_owned entirely — only remap
Scalar::Null variants to the dtype-specific missing marker and pass
everything else through untouched. This avoids redundant work on the
hot path where most values already have the correct type.

fp-types: remove dead identity-cast arms from cast_scalar_owned
(Bool->Bool, Int64->Int64, Float64->Float64, Utf8->Utf8). Since
identity casts are already short-circuited by an early return at the
top of the function, these match arms were unreachable dead code.
Simplify the Utf8 target arm to just return InvalidCast since the
only valid case (Utf8->Utf8) is handled by the early return.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
